stories.title: The School Trip
stories.story:
Every student in Miss Amal's class was excited. Tomorrow, they were going on a school trip to the zoo! That night, Layla couldn't sleep. She kept thinking about the lions and giraffes she would see.

The next day, the whole class got on the big yellow bus. At the zoo, they saw monkeys swinging in the trees and elephants spraying water. It was amazing! But when the class stopped to eat lunch, Layla noticed something terrible. Her best friend, Nada, was missing!

Layla told Miss Amal right away. Everyone looked worried. They searched near the elephants, near the gift shop, and near the ice cream stand. Finally, they found Nada by the bird cage, watching a colorful parrot. She had wandered off just to get a closer look.

"You must always stay with the group," Miss Amal said gently. Nada nodded, a little embarrassed. For the rest of the day, she held Layla's hand tightly, and they finished the trip together, laughing about the funny parrot that had caused all the excitement.

stories.title: Grandma's Garden
stories.story:
Every Friday, Khaled visited his grandmother's house. She had a big garden full of tomatoes, cucumbers, and colorful flowers. At first, Khaled thought gardening was boring. He would rather play video games.

One Friday, Grandma asked him to help her plant new seeds. "Gardening takes patience," she said, showing him how to dig small holes. "You plant the seed, you water it, and then you wait." Khaled didn't understand why anyone would enjoy waiting for so long.

Weeks passed. Every Friday, Khaled watered the little plants with Grandma. Slowly, tiny green leaves started to appear. Khaled found himself checking on his plants even when it wasn't Friday!

Finally, after two months, the tomatoes were ready. Khaled picked the biggest, reddest one and took a bite. It tasted better than any tomato from the shop. "I grew this myself," he said proudly. Grandma smiled. "Now you understand," she said. "Good things take time." From then on, Khaled asked to visit the garden even on days that weren't Friday.
